turning_point_count: How many local {turning_point_plural} are present? A local {turning_point_type} has a value {turning_point_comparison} the immediately previous and next labels in displayed order.
Count the local {turning_point_plural} in displayed order. A local {turning_point_type} is {turning_point_comparison} both adjacent labels.
How many labels are local {turning_point_plural} when you follow the chart in displayed order?
Determine the number of local {turning_point_plural}. Each one is {turning_point_comparison} the labels immediately before and after it in displayed order.
Find how many local {turning_point_plural} the ordered chart contains in displayed order.
longest_monotone_streak: What is the length of the longest strictly {streak_direction} streak of consecutive labels in displayed order? Count labels in the streak.
Find the number of labels in the longest strictly {streak_direction} consecutive streak.
Determine the length of the longest streak where each consecutive label {streak_step_verb} in value. Count labels, not gaps.
How many labels are in the longest strictly {streak_direction} streak in displayed order?
Compute the length of the longest strictly {streak_direction} run of consecutive labels.
endpoint_change_value: Using "{start_label}" as the first endpoint and "{end_label}" as the second endpoint, compute the requested {endpoint_change_kind} change value.
Read the endpoint values at "{start_label}" and "{end_label}"; what is the requested {endpoint_change_kind} change?
Compute the requested endpoint-change value from "{start_label}" to "{end_label}".
Use endpoint labels "{start_label}" and "{end_label}" to compute the requested {endpoint_change_kind} change value.
interval_rate_value: What is the absolute average change per step from "{start_label}" through "{end_label}"?
Compute the nonnegative absolute change from "{start_label}" to "{end_label}" divided by the number of steps between them.
Find the absolute average per-label-step change over the interval from "{start_label}" to "{end_label}".
Find the nonnegative integer average rate of change from "{start_label}" to "{end_label}".
Using endpoint labels "{start_label}" and "{end_label}", compute the absolute average change per step.
first_crosses_above_threshold: Which label is the first one in displayed order with value greater than {threshold}?
Following the displayed order, find the first label whose value is above {threshold}.
What is the first label where the chart value crosses above threshold {threshold}?
Identify the earliest displayed label with value greater than {threshold}.
Starting from the first label, which label first exceeds {threshold}?
first_crosses_below_threshold: Which label is the first one in displayed order with value less than {threshold}?
Following the displayed order, find the first label whose value is below {threshold}.
What is the first label where the chart value crosses below threshold {threshold}?
Identify the earliest displayed label with value less than {threshold}.
Starting from the first label, which label first goes below {threshold}?
threshold_crossing: {crossing_mode_instruction} Which label is first with value {comparison_phrase} {threshold}? {unanswerable_instruction}
Use threshold {threshold}. {crossing_mode_instruction} Identify the first label that goes {crossing_direction} {threshold}. {unanswerable_instruction}
{crossing_mode_instruction} What is the first label whose value crosses {crossing_direction} threshold {threshold}? {unanswerable_instruction}
{crossing_mode_instruction} Find the earliest label with value {comparison_phrase} {threshold}. {unanswerable_instruction}
Use the threshold rule {comparison_phrase} {threshold}. {crossing_mode_instruction} Which label satisfies it first? {unanswerable_instruction}
linear_projection_crosses_above: The plotted labels form one linear series, and the final labels are empty future slots. Continue the same step; which future label is first greater than {threshold}?
Use the repeated step in the plotted linear series to extend into the empty future labels. Which future label first rises above {threshold}?
Following the empty future labels, what is the first label whose extrapolated linear-series value is greater than {threshold}?
Extend the shown linear trend into the future labels. Which future label first exceeds {threshold}?
Among the empty future labels, identify the first one whose continued linear-series value crosses above {threshold}.
linear_projection_crosses_below: The plotted labels form one linear series, and the final labels are empty future slots. Continue the same step; which future label is first less than {threshold}?
Use the repeated step in the plotted linear series to extend into the empty future labels. Which future label first falls below {threshold}?
Following the empty future labels, what is the first label whose extrapolated linear-series value is less than {threshold}?
Extend the shown linear trend into the future labels. Which future label first goes below {threshold}?
Among the empty future labels, identify the first one whose continued linear-series value crosses below {threshold}.
absolute_change_between_labels: What is the absolute change in value from "{start_label}" to "{end_label}"?
Find the absolute difference between the values at "{start_label}" and "{end_label}".
How much did the value change in absolute terms from "{start_label}" to "{end_label}"?
Find the nonnegative change between "{start_label}" and "{end_label}".
signed_change_between_labels: What is the signed change in value from "{start_label}" to "{end_label}"? Use value at "{end_label}" minus value at "{start_label}".
Compute value at "{end_label}" minus value at "{start_label}"; keep the sign.
How much did the value change from "{start_label}" to "{end_label}", using a negative answer for a decrease?
Find the signed endpoint difference from "{start_label}" to "{end_label}".
Find the change from "{start_label}" to "{end_label}", preserving sign.
percent_change_between_labels: What is the signed integer percentage change from "{start_label}" to "{end_label}"? Use ((end-start)/start)*100, keep negative values for decreases, and omit the percent sign.
Compute the signed percent change from "{start_label}" to "{end_label}"; use the integer percentage without a percent sign, negative if it decreased.
Using "{start_label}" as the base, what is the signed integer percent change at "{end_label}"? Use a negative value for a decrease.
Find 100 times the signed change divided by the value at "{start_label}"; keep the sign and omit the percent sign.
Find the signed integer percentage change from "{start_label}" to "{end_label}", using a negative answer for a decrease.
average_rate_over_interval: What is the absolute average change per step from "{start_label}" through "{end_label}"?
Compute the nonnegative absolute change from "{start_label}" to "{end_label}" divided by the number of steps between them.
Find the absolute average per-label-step change over the interval from "{start_label}" to "{end_label}".

Using endpoint labels "{start_label}" and "{end_label}", compute the absolute average change per step.

global.json_output_contract: Use a valid JSON object with keys "annotation" and "answer" in that order for the final answer.
global.json_output_contract_answer_only: Use a valid JSON object with key "answer" for the final answer.

query:peak_turning_point_count.answer_hint: set "answer" to the requested count as an integer
query:peak_turning_point_count.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to an array of [x,y] pixel points on all local turning points of the requested type

query:trough_turning_point_count.answer_hint: set "answer" to the requested count as an integer
query:trough_turning_point_count.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to an array of [x,y] pixel points on all local turning points of the requested type

query:longest_increasing_streak_length.answer_hint: set "answer" to the requested streak length as an integer
query:longest_increasing_streak_length.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to an array of [x,y] pixel points on every mark in the unique longest matching monotone streak

query:longest_decreasing_streak_length.answer_hint: set "answer" to the requested streak length as an integer
query:longest_decreasing_streak_length.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to an array of [x,y] pixel points on every mark in the unique longest matching monotone streak

query:absolute_endpoint_change_value.answer_hint: set "answer" to the requested endpoint-change value as an integer
query:absolute_endpoint_change_value.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to an object mapping "start_mark" and "end_mark" to [x,y] pixel points on the two endpoint marks

query:signed_endpoint_change_value.answer_hint: set "answer" to the requested endpoint-change value as an integer
query:signed_endpoint_change_value.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to an object mapping "start_mark" and "end_mark" to [x,y] pixel points on the two endpoint marks

query:percent_endpoint_change_value.answer_hint: set "answer" to the requested signed integer percentage change, omitting the percent sign and using a negative value for a decrease
query:percent_endpoint_change_value.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to an object mapping "start_mark" and "end_mark" to [x,y] pixel points on the two endpoint marks

query:average_rate_over_interval.answer_hint: set "answer" to the requested nonnegative absolute average rate as an integer
query:average_rate_over_interval.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to an object mapping "start_mark" and "end_mark" to [x,y] pixel points on the two endpoint marks

query:observed_above_threshold_crossing_.answer_hint: set "answer" to the requested crossing label as a string
query:observed_above_threshold_crossing_.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to one [x,y] pixel point on the first threshold-crossing mark

query:observed_below_threshold_crossing_.answer_hint: set "answer" to the requested crossing label as a string
query:observed_below_threshold_crossing_.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to one [x,y] pixel point on the first threshold-crossing mark
